description: Beneath the surface where sunlight is a memory. Caverns, tunnels, ruins, and stranger places carved by water, fire, or hands long forgotten.
locations: Underground River
Black water rushes through stone, cold and fast. The current echoes off a low ceiling, and anything that falls in is gone.
Cavern Lake
Still water stretches into darkness, mirror-smooth and impossibly deep. Pale things move beneath the surface when the torchlight hits it wrong.
Dropoff
The floor just ends. Wind rises from the void below, carrying the smell of wet stone. A kicked pebble falls for a very long time.
Cavern
A cathedral of raw stone opens overhead. Dripping water counts the silence. The dark presses close beyond the reach of any light.
Tunnels
Branching passages wind deeper without pattern or logic. Scratches along the walls might be directions, or warnings.
Goblin Camp
Firelight flickers against low walls of scrap and bone. The smell of rancid fat hits before the noise does. Small eyes watch from every shadow.
Spider Nest
Silk threads catch the light between the stones. The webs grow thicker the deeper you go. Something large is patient here.
Well Bottom
A circle of sky far above, impossibly small. The walls are slick with moss, the water is knee-deep and cold, and the rope is gone.
Underdark Cavern
Bioluminescent fungi paint the stone in bruise-purple light. The air is thick and ancient. Civilizations thrive down here that have never seen the sun.
Undead Barrows
The earth mounds are gouged open from the inside. Rusted armor litters the ground. At night the scratching from below begins again.
Crypt
Cold stone and the sweet rot of centuries. Names worn smooth on the walls. Some sarcophagi are open, and not all were opened from outside.
Stairways
Worn steps spiral downward, carved long ago by hands that knew where they led. The air grows colder with each turn.
Catacombs
Bones stacked floor to ceiling in careful rows, thousands of the nameless dead. The deeper corridors are less organized, as if arranged in haste.
Tomb
Sealed air and dust older than memory. Gold gleams in the torchlight, untouched. The dead were buried with everything they valued, and something to guard it.
Mine Gallery
Timber braces groan under the weight of the mountain. Ore veins glint in the walls, half-harvested. The miners left their tools behind.
Mine Shaft
A vertical drop into blackness, ladders bolted to the rock with rusting iron. The air rising from below is warm and wrong.
Sewer
Fetid water moves slow through arched stone corridors. Rats scatter at the light. Whole communities survive down here, hidden from the world above.
Labyrinth
Every passage looks the same. The walls are too smooth, too deliberate. This place was built to keep something in, not to keep visitors out.
Abyss
The edge of the world, straight down. Darkness so deep it feels alive. Stones thrown over the edge never make a sound.
Ice Cave
Frozen air burns the lungs. Blue light filters through walls of ancient ice, and shapes are trapped inside, preserved and watching.
Gem Mines
Raw crystals jut from every surface, catching light in fractured rainbows. The wealth here is obvious. So are the pickaxe gouges that stopped suddenly.
Forgotten Fault
A crack in the earth's bones, jagged and deep. Hot air rises from below, carrying the smell of sulfur and something older.
Cistern
Vaulted ceilings echo above dark water. Stone columns march into the gloom, waist-deep in the reservoir. The city it once served is gone.
Crystal Caverns
Every surface refracts light into scattered rainbows. The crystals hum at a frequency felt in the teeth. Some of them pulse with their own warmth.
Lava Tubes
Smooth-walled tunnels shaped by ancient fire. The stone is still warm to the touch, and a faint orange glow pulses from deeper passages.
Underground Hot Springs
Steam curls off mineral-blue water in a stone basin. The warmth is a gift in the deep cold. Tracks in the mud say others know this place.
Mushroom Grove
Towering fungi rise like pale trees, their caps wide enough to shelter under. Spores drift in the still air, glowing faintly gold.
Stalactite Chamber
Stone teeth hang from the ceiling, some thin as needles, others thick as pillars. Water drips in a slow rhythm. One wrong sound brings them down.
Underground Waterfall
The roar fills the cavern before you see it. White water crashes from a fissure high above into a churning pool. The mist soaks everything.
Limestone Caves
Pale stone shaped by patient water over ages. Smooth columns and frozen curtains of rock. Beautiful and disorienting, every chamber looks like the last.
Mineral Pools
Terraced basins of vivid color step down a cavern slope. The water is warm and alkaline. Strange things calcify at the edges.
Bat Colony
The ceiling writhes. Thousands of leather wings shift and chitter overhead. The guano is ankle-deep, the ammonia sharp enough to water the eyes.
Underground Chasm
A rift splits the cavern floor, too wide to jump. Rope bridges sway over the gap, fraying. The far side holds something worth the crossing.
Root Cellar
Packed earth walls and the smell of damp soil. Barrels and crates crowd the small space. A trapdoor in the floor leads somewhere unexpected.
Natural Amphitheater
A bowl of stone shaped by water and time, with perfect acoustics. Whispers from the bottom carry to the rim. Someone has gathered here before.
Geothermal Vents
The ground hisses and spits. Mineral-crusted fissures exhale scalding steam at unpredictable intervals. Strange life clusters near the vents, thriving on the heat.
Dwarven Halls
Grand columns carved with ancestral faces hold up a mountain. The stonework is flawless, the silence absolute. The builders are long gone but the forges still hold heat.
Ancient Library
Shelves carved into living rock, filled with texts in dead languages. The air is dry as dust to preserve the knowledge. Some volumes are chained shut.
Temple Ruins
Broken altars and toppled idols in a vast underground chamber. The worship stopped long ago, but the presence it called to lingers in the cold.
Treasure Vault
Iron doors scarred with failed attempts to breach them. Inside, gold catches the light in heaps. The traps that guard it are still alive.
Ritual Chamber
Dark stains on the stone floor form precise circles. Candle niches line the walls. The air tastes metallic, and sound behaves strangely here.
Dragon's Lair
The heat hits first, then the smell of charred stone and old bone. Gold coins carpet the floor of a cavern scored by claw marks the size of a man.
Troll Caves
A reek of carrion and damp fur pours from the entrance. Gnawed bones litter the threshold. The growling from inside never quite stops.
Owlbear Den
Feathers and fur mat the cave floor alongside cracked bones. Deep gouges scar the walls. The owner is out hunting, but not for long.
Kobold Warrens
Cramped tunnels branch in every direction, sized for smaller bodies. Crude traps line every passage. The warrens go deeper and wider than they first appear.
Mind Flayer Colony
The walls glisten with mucus. A low psychic hum presses against the skull, growing stronger ahead. Thralls shuffle through the corridors, blank-eyed and obedient.
Beholder Sanctum
A perfectly spherical chamber, carved by disintegration rays. Petrified victims line the walls like trophies. The central darkness watches from every angle at once.
Purple Worm Tunnels
Perfectly round corridors bored through solid rock, wide enough to march an army through. The stone is glazed smooth. Tremors say the digger is close.
Giant Ant Colony
Geometric tunnels branch with alien precision. The chitinous scraping of countless legs echoes from every direction. Workers haul cargo twice their size without pause.
Orc Stronghold
Crude iron and heavy timber brace the cavern walls. War drums echo from deeper in. The defenses are brutal but effective, built by those who expect a fight.
Underground Market
Lanterns sway over stalls carved into the rock. Traders hawk wares that would get them hanged on the surface. Coin talks, and questions are unwelcome.
Smuggler's Den
Hidden behind a waterfall, crates stamped with foreign seals fill every alcove. Maps cover a makeshift table. Someone is running a careful operation.
Thieves' Hideout
A hollowed-out space behind false walls, furnished with stolen comforts. Escape routes branch in three directions. The occupants know these tunnels better than anyone.
Forgotten Shrine
Offerings of tarnished silver sit before a faceless idol. The faith that built this place is extinct, but the prayers left an imprint that still hums.
Wizard's Laboratory
Glass vessels bubble over cold flames. Chalk circles cover the floor, some half-erased in haste. The experiments left behind are still reacting.
Enchanted Grotto
The water glows from within. Flowers bloom in soil that has never seen sunlight. The magic here is old and wild, belonging to no one.
Portal Chamber
Runes circle a stone platform, pulsing with residual energy. The air between the pillars shimmers and bends. Wherever this connects to, the door is not fully closed.
Scrying Pool
Ink-black water in a stone basin, utterly still. Stare long enough and the surface shows places far away. Not everything it reveals is true.
Elemental Node
Raw power bleeds from the stone. The air crackles, the ground shifts, and flames lick from cracks without fuel. The boundary between planes is thin here.
Ley Line Convergence
The hairs stand on end. Magic saturates the air so thickly that spells cast here are unpredictable and amplified. Something is always listening.
Dimensional Rift
Reality frays at the edges. Colors bleed wrong, gravity shifts without warning, and the passage ahead opens onto a sky that belongs to no known world.
Prison Cells
Iron bars thick with rust line a narrow corridor. Scratched tallies cover the walls. Some doors hang open. Others are still locked, with something breathing inside.
Torture Chamber
Stained iron implements hang from hooks on the wall. The drains in the floor tell their own story. The echoes here carry sounds from years past.
Armory
Racks of weapons line the walls, some mundane, some radiating faint warmth. Dust coats everything except one empty stand where something was recently taken.
Wine Cellar
Oak barrels rest in cool darkness, their contents aging in silence. The oldest vintages sit deeper in, behind a locked gate with no visible key.
Storage Vault
Heavy stone doors guard crates and chests stacked to the ceiling. Inventory lists nailed to the wall are written in a cipher no one remembers.
Underground Barracks
Rows of stone bunks carved into the walls, blankets still folded. Mess kits and personal effects sit untouched. The garrison left in formation and never came back.
Smithy Forge
Heat radiates from a forge built into a volcanic fissure. Anvils and quenching troughs stand ready. The metal on the workbench is like nothing found on the surface.
Alchemist's Workshop
Bubbling flasks and coiled tubing fill shelves carved into the rock. The air stings with chemical fumes. A journal lies open to a page marked 'do not attempt.'
Underground Spring
Clear water wells up from bare stone, cold and sweet. The source is deep and steady. Someone carved a basin to catch it, long ago.
Fossil Beds
Ancient bones jut from exposed stone, creatures from an age before memory. Some are impossibly large. A few seem to belong to things that should not have existed.
Salt Deposits
White crystal crusts every surface, glittering in the light. The air is dry and sharp. Carved chambers open into vast pillared halls of pure salt.
Underground Gorge
A narrow slash through the stone, walls close enough to touch on both sides. The river at the bottom carved this over millennia and still runs fast.
Sinkhole
The ground collapsed here, leaving a raw wound in the earth. Broken roots and shattered stone frame a drop into chambers never meant to be found.
Underground Cliff
The passage opens onto a sheer drop. A narrow ledge traces the cliff face, slick with moisture. The cavern below stretches further than the light can reach.
Flooded Chambers
Dark water fills the rooms to the doorframes. Furniture bobs gently in the still flood. Whatever is stored here sits submerged and waiting below the waterline.
Quicksand Pits
The floor looks solid but gives like wet clay underfoot. Bones protrude from the sandy patches, half-swallowed. The firm ground between the pits is narrow.
Underground Desert
Impossible dunes of fine sand fill a cavern so vast the ceiling vanishes. The air is bone-dry and hot. Wind moves through here from no visible source.
Bone Yard
Skeletons of every size carpet the cavern floor, piled deep. The bones are old and dry, but arranged too neatly to be natural.
Spider Tunnels
Webs coat every surface, thick as curtains. Desiccated husks hang in silk bundles along the walls. The vibrations of each footstep travel far.
Myconid Colony
Towering mushroom-folk stand in silent circles, communicating in clouds of spores. The air is thick and sweet. They tolerate visitors, but their patience has limits.
Deep Gnome Village
Tiny stone houses cluster around a phosphorescent pool. The residents watch from doorways with wide, dark-adapted eyes. Trust is earned slowly this far down.
Dark Elf Outpost
Obsidian architecture rises in cruel elegance. Faint violet light illuminates patrols moving with predatory grace. Outsiders are noticed immediately and studied from the shadows.
Underground Arena
Tiered stone seating surrounds a sand-floored pit stained dark with old blood. Weapons are chained to the walls. The crowd that fills these seats expects a show.
Gladiator Pits
Iron cages line a torch-lit corridor. The fighters inside size up every newcomer. Victory buys another day, and the crowd above is already chanting.
Sacrificial Altar
A black stone slab sits at the center of concentric circles. The grooves carved into its surface lead to a basin that has never been cleaned.
Necromancer's Sanctum
The cold here is unnatural, settling in the bones. Shelves of labeled jars hold things best left unnamed. The occupant's servants stand motionless until commanded.
Lich's Phylactery Chamber
Wards shimmer across every surface. Protective magic so dense it resists movement fills the air. Something precious is kept here, defended by centuries of paranoia.
Vampire Crypt
Silk and velvet line a stone coffin in a chamber that smells of earth and old perfume. The candles are fresh. Someone returns here before dawn.
Ghost-Haunted Halls
The temperature plummets. Breath fogs the air, and voices whisper from nowhere, overlapping and urgent. The dead here have something to say and no rest until it is heard.
Spectral Ruins
Translucent walls shimmer where solid ones once stood. The ghostly architecture rebuilds and crumbles in a silent loop, replaying a destruction that happened long ago.
Cursed Burial Ground
The soil is sour. Nothing grows here, not even fungus. The graves are shallow and restless. Disturbing this place is easy. Leaving it alone is wiser.
Underground Garden
Bioluminescent blooms light a cavern tended by no one. Vines climb stone trellises and fruit hangs heavy from pale branches. The beauty feels deliberate and watched.
Fungal Forest
Mushrooms tall as oaks crowd a vast cavern, their caps forming a canopy. Spores drift like pollen in lazy currents. The ecosystem here answers to its own rules.
Glowing Moss Cavern
Soft green light coats every surface, cast by living moss. The glow pulses gently, like breathing. It is quiet here and warm, and easy to lose track of time.
Phosphorescent Pools
The water shines pale blue when disturbed. Each ripple sends light dancing across the cavern ceiling. Small things dart beneath the glow.
Echo Chambers
Every sound returns tenfold. A whisper becomes a chorus, a footstep becomes thunder. Navigating by sound is impossible here. Something uses that to its advantage.
Whispering Galleries
Curved stone walls carry voices from far-off chambers with perfect clarity. Conversations not meant to be heard arrive whole and clear. Secrets do not survive here.
Singing Crystals
Crystalline formations vibrate with harmonic tones that shift as air moves through the cavern. The melody is hauntingly beautiful and never quite repeats.
Resonance Cavern
A deep hum fills the chamber, felt more than heard. The frequency shifts with movement and makes the bones ache. Staying too long brings nosebleeds and strange visions.
Stone Archway
A carved arch frames the passage ahead, decorated with warnings in a dead language. Whatever lies beyond warranted a formal threshold.
Collapsed Tunnel
Rubble chokes the passage, but gaps between the stones let air through from the other side. Squeezing through is possible. Getting stuck is also possible.
Cave-In Site
Shattered timber and crushed stone block what was once a wide corridor. Dust still hangs in the air. The ceiling above looks ready to give again.
Unstable Ceiling
Cracks web across the stone overhead. Pebbles fall without warning, and the timber braces are bowed under the weight. Every loud noise is a gamble.
Gas Vents
The air shimmers with invisible fumes. The smell is faint and sweet, which makes it worse. Open flame is a terrible idea here.
Poison Pools
Still water with an oily rainbow sheen fills shallow depressions in the floor. Dead insects ring the edges. The air above the surface burns the nostrils.
Acid Springs
Water bubbles up from the stone, clear but caustic. The rocks around the spring are eaten smooth and pale. Metal corrodes in minutes here.
Boiling Mud Pots
Thick grey mud bubbles and belches in shallow craters. The heat is intense and the sulfur smell is overpowering. The ground between the pots is thin.
Underground Geyser
A column of scalding water erupts from the stone at intervals, hitting the ceiling and raining back down. The pattern seems regular, but counting on it is risky.
Steam Vents
Jets of white vapor hiss from fissures in the floor, obscuring the path. The heat is punishing. Visibility drops to nothing when the vents pulse together.
Thermal Pools
Warm water fills stone basins, tinted mineral-blue and steaming gently. The heat soothes aching muscles. A rare comfort this deep, if you trust the silence around it.
Mine
Timber braces groan and ore glints in the walls. The tracks lead deeper, the air gets warmer, and the pickaxes left behind still have fresh edges.
